title: KeyPackageReady broadcast after DB write — happens-before edge is correct
description: ws.rs broadcasts KeyPackageReady AFTER save_key_package() succeeds. A contributor might suggest broadcasting immediately (before the await) to reduce latency. Do not do that. The async SQLite write may not have committed at that point; a concurrent GetKeyPackage would return None, silently failing to add the new member with no error and no retry. The broadcast-after-write creates a happens-before edge guaranteeing any GetKeyPackage sent in response finds the data. The latency cost (one SQLite write roundtrip, <1 ms) is worth the correctness guarantee.
close_reason: informational review note — design confirmed correct, no action needed
